project: Learning-Based MPC Leveraging SINDy for Vehicle Dynamics Estimation

reference_paper.title: Learning-Based MPC Leveraging SINDy for Vehicle Dynamics Estimation
reference_paper.authors: Paparazzo, Castoldi, Sathyamangalam Imran, Arrigoni, Braghin

overall_goal: Implement the paper's SINDy-MPC framework in MATLAB: identify a 3-DOF bicycle model from simulation data using SINDy, then use it as the predictive model inside an NMPC controller for autonomous vehicle track following.

stage_1_data_generation.description: Drive a 3-DOF single-track vehicle model around an oval track at multiple speeds, logging states, derivatives, and slip angles for SINDy identification.
known_issues_resolved: Pure pursuit sign was flipped — needed negative sign on feedback term
Pure pursuit alone cannot track curved roads — added curvature feedforward: delta_ff = atan(L_wb * kappa_lookahead)
Track turns R=20m were physically impossible at higher speeds (required >2g lateral accel) — increased to R=80m
P speed controller drove torque to zero at steady state because it had no resistance feedforward — added Tr_ff = Fres * R_wheel as baseline torque
PD derivative term was noisy with dt=0.001 — switched to pure P + feedforward
Vehicle stalled due to vx<0.5 safety check triggering — lowered to vx<0.1
Initial Tr=0 caused speed dip — initialized to resistance-balancing value
stage_1_data_generation.current_state: Last fix added resistance feedforward to speed controller. Needs re-run to verify vehicle completes laps. If slip angles are too small (only ±2-3 deg), data won't cover nonlinear tire region — may need tighter track or chirp excitation later.

stage_2_sindy_identification.description: Build library matrix Theta from logged data, run sequential thresholded least squares (or LASSO) to find sparse coefficients c1-c25 for the 3 dynamic equations.

details.algorithm: Sequential thresholded least squares with sparsity parameter lambda, 10 iterations (NSINDy=10)

stage_3_offline_mpc.description: Build NMPC using CasADi in MATLAB. Predictive model is the full 8-state system (Eq. 16) with c1-c25 as parameters from SINDy. Validate in closed loop against the 3-DOF plant on a different track.

details.solver: CasADi with direct collocation or multiple shooting (paper uses acados with RTI-SQP)

stage_4_online_adaptation.description: Start MPC with intentionally wrong model (perturb coefficients ±20%). Run 2 laps, then activate Constrained-SINDy to re-identify coefficients in real time. Compare lap times and trajectory quality before/after.

details.constrained_sindy: Solve LS minimization with box constraints on coefficients using quadprog: lb = c_offline * 0.8, ub = c_offline * 1.2

dynamic_sindy_identified: vx_dot = c1*omega*vy + c2*Tr + (c3*af + c4*af^3 + c5*af^5 + c6*af^7)*sin(delta) + c7*vx + c8*vx^2
vy_dot = c9*omega*vx + (c10*af + c11*af^3 + c12*af^5 + c13*af^7)*cos(delta) + c14*ar + c15*ar^3 + c16*ar^5 + c17*ar^7
omega_dot = (c18*af + c19*af^3 + c20*af^5 + c21*af^7)*cos(delta) + c22*ar + c23*ar^3 + c24*ar^5 + c25*ar^7

vehicle_model.tire_model: Pacejka Magic Formula: F = D*sin(C*atan(B*alpha - E*(B*alpha - atan(B*alpha))))

sindy_formulation.library_design: Odd polynomial functions of slip angles (alpha, alpha^3, alpha^5, alpha^7) — justified by odd symmetry of Pacejka lateral forces. Physics-informed: only candidate functions with known physical meaning are included.
sindy_formulation.sparsity_promotion: L1 regularization (LASSO) or sequential thresholded LS with parameter lambda
sindy_formulation.constrained_version: For online use with high-fidelity plant: solve as constrained NLP with box constraints on coefficients to prevent unphysical values. Uses quadprog or acados.
sindy_formulation.key_insight: The 25 coefficients make the bicycle model fully tunable. Tire force curves can be reconstructed from identified weights. Vehicle mass can be inferred from c2 (torque coefficient).

steering.feedback: delta_fb = -atan(2 * L_wb * e_la / Ld^2) where e_la = y + Ld * sin(xi)
steering.note: Feedforward is essential — pure pursuit alone cannot anticipate road curvature in curvilinear coordinates

speed.note: Feedforward prevents stalling — without it, P controller drives torque to zero at steady state

data_generation_controller.rate_interface: u1 = K_delta * (delta_des - delta_current), u2 = K_T * (T_des - Tr_current)

track.note: R=80m chosen so all test speeds (10-25 m/s) stay within tire friction limits

next_steps_after_data_gen_works: 1. Verify vehicle completes all laps at all speeds
2. Check slip angle coverage — need ±5-10+ degrees for nonlinear tire identification
3. If slip angles too small, add chirp excitation overlay on steering or use tighter track
4. Implement SINDy: build Theta matrix, run thresholded LS, validate with R^2
5. Reconstruct tire force curves from identified coefficients
6. Build NMPC with CasADi using identified model
7. Test closed-loop MPC on different track
8. Implement online Constrained-SINDy adaptation with ±20% perturbation scenario

user_context.name: Orion
user_context.background: Control systems and robotics engineering student at AUB. Research in active suspension control (QP-based fault-tolerant allocation for 7-DOF full-car model), quadruped locomotion, and data-driven modeling. Currently taking Data-Driven Modeling course covering SINDy, DMD, neural ODEs, PINNs. Exploring VINDy + Stochastic MPC for vehicle dynamics.
user_context.working_preferences: Expects full algorithm logic review and agreement before code changes. Single-file MATLAB preferred over multi-file. Wants clear explanations of design decisions.
user_context.related_work: Has a 7-DOF full-car MATLAB model with QP-based active suspension — future goal is to use that as the plant instead of the 3-DOF model, creating real plant-model mismatch for a more meaningful SINDy identification challenge.
